refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process can be decomposed into data acquisition from sensors and telemetry, and data processing to update the model. This decomposition isolates sources of delay and enables targeted design solutions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency from sensor data generation to worksite model update completion under representative operational scenarios, ensuring p95 latency ≤ 1.0 s.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire sensor and telemetry data with minimal latency.
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry protocols and optimized data acquisition pipelines.
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms with parallelization and incremental model updates.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address data acquisition and processing latency respectively.

potential_sources: Data acquisition and processing are sequential but can be modularized with well-defined interfaces.
Minimal coupling expected if telemetry and processing modules communicate via standardized interfaces.
coupling_check.notes: The two DPs address distinct latency sources and can be designed to minimize interdependency.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of telemetry infrastructure supporting low-latency data transfer.
Risk that processing algorithms may not meet latency targets under peak load.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor processing performance and telemetry reliability; consider fallback or adaptive algorithms if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for integrating telemetry acquisition and data processing to update the worksite model.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and vehicle telemetry data streams for processing.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data communication between vehicle, teleoperation, and NGHE solution modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes data acquisition and processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
Limited containment as vehicle mainly provides raw telemetry; processing is external.
Primarily consumes processed data; not responsible for update latency.
Focuses on safety alerts, not involved in worksite model updates.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the update latency FR to the NGHE Solution module centralizes responsibility and reduces interface complexity, improving latency performance.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded.
Dependency on network link reliability may impact latency.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing NGHE Solution internally if complexity grows; monitor network link performance and implement redundancy if needed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The FR is clearly decomposed into sub-FRs addressing distinct latency sources, and corresponding DPs logically solve these sub-FRs with minimal coupling.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk as the NGHE Solution module encapsulates both data acquisition and processing DPs.
Interfaces are well-defined and limited to known telemetry and network link interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as the best architectural boundary for this FR.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed end-to-end within the NGHE Solution module using telemetry data and latency measurements.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor performance metrics to validate latency assumptions.
Plan for scalability and fault tolerance within NGHE Solution module.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment are consistent, feasible, and meet the latency requirement with clear ownership and verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire sensor and telemetry data with minimal latency.
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Telemetry infrastructure supports required low-latency data transfer.
Processing algorithms can be optimized to meet latency targets.
open_risks: Processing load spikes causing latency violations.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data from prototype or simulation.
